info.name: LMS — Books (v1)
info.description:
Student / teacher app APIs for class books (PDF library).

## Auto-scoping by role

The SAME endpoint serves both roles — the back-end derives what to return from the Sanctum token, no role parameter needed.

- **Student** (`role=user`) → only books for **their class + section**.
  Books with `section_id = NULL` (class-wide / shared books) are also included for the student's class.
- **Teacher** (`role=teacher`) → only books for the **(class, subject) pairs they teach**.
  Those pairs are derived from the **teacher timetable** (`teacher_time_tables`).

## Card payload

Every list item already carries everything both card UIs need + the `pdf_url` for tap-to-open, so the front-end does NOT need a second `/books/{id}` call just to read.

**Student card needs**: `subject.name`, `cover_url`, `title`, `pdf_url`.
**Teacher card needs**: `subject.name`, `standard.name`, `section.name`, `title`, `cover_url`, `pdf_url`.

`logo_url` is a backwards-compatible alias of `cover_url` — old clients keep working.

## Response envelope

All responses are wrapped: `{ success, status_code, message, data }`. The **list** endpoint paginates inside `data`: `data.items` is the array and `data.pagination` carries `{ total, per_page, current_page, last_page, from, to }`. (The single-book endpoint returns the book object directly in `data`.)

## In-app PDF reader

The app opens `pdf_url` in an in-app reader screen (no external browser) that supports **go to page** — the reader can jump straight to a typed page number. Both the student and teacher book screens use the same reader.

## Auth

Sanctum bearer token in collection variable `authToken`.

query.description: Optional. Narrow to a specific class within the caller's scope.
Optional. Narrow to a specific section within the caller's scope.
Optional. Narrow to a specific subject within the caller's scope.
Optional. Case-insensitive substring match against book title.

request.description:
Paginated list of active books, auto-scoped to the caller's role.

Each item is shaped to render a card directly AND open the PDF without a second call.

Fields:
- `id` — book id
- `title` — book name (card heading)
- `cover_url` — cover image (card image)
- `logo_url` — same as `cover_url` (back-compat alias)
- `subject` — `{ id, name, image }` — `image` is the subject icon (full S3 URL or null) shown on the book card; the app falls back to a book icon when it's null
- `standard` — `{ id, name }` — class (used by teacher card; omitted on student UI)
- `section` — `{ id, name }` — section (used by teacher card; omitted on student UI)
- `pdf_url` — direct PDF target for the card's "open" tap

response.name: Sample — Student (role=user)
Sample — Teacher (role=teacher)

request.description:
Single book by id, still role-scoped — returns the same shape as a list item.

Most clients no longer need this endpoint because the list response already carries `pdf_url`. It remains available for deep-link / share scenarios where only the book id is known.

- 200 → book payload (same fields as list item)
- 404 → book is outside the caller's scope or doesn't exist
